data.title: April is Women's Eye Health and Safety Month at Prevent Blindness, Aimed at Providing Women with Resources Needed to Help Save Vision
data.description: Prevent Blindness advocates for equal access to eye care and provides free educational resources as women face a higher risk of sight-threatening eye diseases. Vision changes during pregnancy and menopause, along with other vision issues, pose significant risks. The organization, based in Chicago, aims to raise awareness about these concerns.

data.title: Linear Health Sciences Names Renowned Cardiac Surgeon and Healthcare Innovator as Chief Medical Officer
data.description: A renowned figure in the medical field, Dr. Robert C. Robbins, M.D., has recently been appointed as a board member and Chief at Linear Health Sciences, based in Oklahoma City. With his extensive clinical experience and expertise in commercialization, Dr. Robbins is set to contribute significantly to the company's growth and development. Linear Health Sciences, known as the world's largest medical innovation ecosystem, is likely to benefit from Dr. Robbins' profound knowledge and leadership. This strategic move is expected to enhance the company's operations and further solidify its position in the industry.

data.title: BayCare's Graduate Medical Education Program Doubles in Size After Successful 2026 Match Day
data.description: In Clearwater, Florida, on March 26, 2026, it was announced that BayCare's Graduate Medical Education program achieved a significant milestone by filling all (100%) of its available residency positions for the second consecutive year. This successful outcome was a result of a fruitful Match Day on March 20, where the program expanded its size by nearly twofold. BayCare, a prominent healthcare provider in the region, has seen remarkable growth and interest in its residency program, solidifying its position as a top choice for medical professionals seeking advanced training opportunities.

data.title: The Management Board of AB “Ignitis grupė” has been elected
data.description: AB “Ignitis grupė” has appointed a new Management Board following the expiration of the previous board's term on March 25, 2026. The Supervisory Board made the decision to elect a new Management Board on March 26, 2026. The Chair of the Management Board was chosen during the first meeting of the new board.

data.title: Kvika banki hf.: Announcement regarding reduction in share capital
data.description: During Kvika's Annual General Meeting on March 18, 2026, it was agreed to decrease the company's share capital by ISK 301,000,000, equivalent to 301,000,000 shares. This reduction brings the total from ISK 4,631,000,000 to ISK 4,330,000,000 by eliminating the company's ownership of 301,000,000 shares.

data.title: Retail Investors Increased Use of Defensive Options Strategies Ahead of Iran Conflict, Data Highlights
data.description: Data from Investa reveals that retail investors in the UK have significantly raised their investments in defensive put options in anticipation of geopolitical tensions. This increase in defensive put options indicates a move towards more cautious investment strategies, with a notable spike in activity among retail investors. The data highlights a growing concern among investors about potential risks in the market, prompting them to take steps to protect their investments. This trend reflects a shifting sentiment in the investment landscape, with investors actively seeking ways to safeguard their portfolios amidst uncertain geopolitical developments.

data.title: Continued Strong Growth at Gilat Telecom: Net profit surged by over 250% to approximately $6.35 million
data.description: Revenue saw a substantial increase to around $75.8 million, with free cash flow rising to $7.8 million. The backlog now surpasses $52 million. The company is solidifying a strong competitive edge in the telecommunications sector and is beginning 2026 in a position of strength due to this development.

data.title: FIRST LOOK: Grand Geneva Resort & Spa’s New Short Course, Wee Nip
data.description: In Lake Geneva, Wisconsin, the Grand Geneva Resort & Spa is unveiling a new 11-hole short course named Wee Nip on May 19, 2026. This resort, known for its AAA Four-Diamond rating, aims to offer a unique golfing experience that promotes a relaxed and social atmosphere. Designed by the acclaimed Dusenberry Design, Wee Nip is crafted to be both inviting and challenging, catering to golfers of varying skill levels. The course emphasizes creativity, strategy, and enjoyment, making it a must-visit destination for golf enthusiasts. Total holes: 11Opening date: May 19, 2026Resort rating: AAA Four-Diamond

data.title: Changes in the management board of Baltic Horizon Capital AS
data.description: The management company of Baltic Horizon Fund, Baltic Horizon Capital AS, has announced updates in its management board.

data.title: Insilico Medicine and Tenacia Biotechnology Expand AI-Driven CNS Collaboration with Deal Value Up to US$94.75 Million
data.description: In Cambridge, Massachusetts on March 26, 2026, Insilico Medicine (3696.HK) - a biotechnology company utilizing generative artificial intelligence and currently in the clinical stage - has announced a partnership with Tenacia Biotechnology (Hong Kong) Co., Limited. Tenacia is a biopharmaceutical company in the commercial stage.

data.title: CPSC Posts Product Safety Warnings to its Web Site
data.description: On March 26, 2026, the Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) released safety warnings regarding Whomec Bed Rails, urging consumers to stop using them immediately. The CPSC emphasized the risk of serious injury or death associated with these products. Consumers can access product safety warnings and recalls at www.cpsc.gov for more information. It is crucial for individuals to heed these warnings to ensure their safety.
